sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good progress. Now I have orange, purple filled in the red frame. The 3rd position has the charcoal square, and the 4th is still gray.
Top order: orange, purple, red, blue, green, yellow, pink
I need to figure out what to do with the 3rd position (charcoal). Maybe I should skip it and fill the 4th with red, then continue with the green frame.
Actually wait - looking at the image, the 3rd square in the red frame is NOT charcoal - it's the green T shape's top part! The green square is at position 3. So the red frame has: orange, purple, [green T top], gray.
The green T is part of the puzzle structure, not a fillable slot. So the red frame has 3 fillable slots (positions 1, 2, 4) and the green frame has 4 slots.
Total: 7 slots = 7 colors. That makes sense!
So the order would be:
Red frame: orange (1), purple (2), [green T], red (4)
Green frame: blue (1), green (2), yellow (3), pink (4)
Let me fill the 4th position in red frame with red.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Fill 4th position in red frame with red (skip position 3 which has green T)
# Red bottom square at [57,9]-[60,12], center ~[58, 10]
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 10}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
